references.text:
[Ad22] Adenwalla, S., Avoiding Monotone Arithmetic Progressions in Permutations of Integers. arXiv:2211.04451 (2022).
 
 [Ge19] Geneson, Jesse, Forbidden arithmetic progressions in permutations of subsets
of the integers. Discrete Math. (2019), 1489-1491.

remarks.text:
Geneson \cite{Ge19} proved that $k\leq 5$. Adenwalla \cite{Ad22} proved that $k\leq 4$.

See also [194] and [196].

statement.text: What is the largest $k$ such that in any permutation of $\mathbb{Z}$ there must exist a monotone $k$-term arithmetic progression $x_1<\cdots<x_k$?
